model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Profile link in the dropdown menu
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=12 dy=323 conf=0.80 -> (650,447)
  iter 1: at (650,447) said dx=0 dy=-112 conf=0.80 -> (650,335)
  iter 2: at (650,391) said dx=0 dy=-40 conf=1.00 -> (650,351)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "Profile" link in the dropdown menu.
The current red crosshair is at (650, 381) based on the text in the bottom right corner, though the prompt history says it was at (650, 351).
Looking at the image, the red crosshair is centered on the "Profile" link.
Wait, looking closely at the image, the crosshair is on the "Profile" text.
The "Profile" text is at approximately x=512, y=495? No, that's not right.
Let's re-examine the coordinates.
The image is 1280x720 (standard for these tasks).
The "Profile" link center is roughly at (512, 495) in a 1280x720 image? No, let's look at the crosshair.
The crosshair is at (650, 381).
The "Profile" text is just above the crosshair? No, the crosshair is right on the text.
Actually, the crosshair is slightly below the "Profile" text and above "Settings".
Wait, let's look again.
The "Profile" text center seems to be around (512, 495) if the image was different.
Let's use the provided coordinates.
The red crosshair is at (650, 381).
The "Profile" link center is roughly where the crosshair is, maybe a bit higher.
Let's look at the crosshair again. It's right in the middle of the blue circle.
The text "Profile" is above the crosshair.
Actually, the crosshair is sitting right on the word "Profile".
No, looking closer, "Profile" is the first item. "Settings" is the second.
The crosshair is between "Profile" and "Settings".
The "Profile" text is centered around (512, 495) in normalized coordinates? No.
Let's estimate coordinates in pixels for a 1280x720 image.
The central box is from x~350 to x~930 and y~250 to y~750.
Wait, the image is 1280x720.
The box center is at 640, 360 approx.
"Profile" is at roughly (512, 495) normalized? No.
Let's re-read the image.
The crosshair is at (650, 381).
The text "Profile" is above the crosshair.
The text "Settings" is below the crosshair.
Actually, the crosshair is almost exactly on "Profile".
Wait, the "Profile" text is the top item in that small menu.
The crosshair is at (650, 381).
The text "Profile" is located at approx x=512 (normalized) -> 655 pixels.
The y coordinate for "Profile" is approx 495 (normalized) -> 356 pixels.
If the crosshair is at (650, 381), then dx = 655 - 650 = 5, dy = 356 - 381 = -25.
Let's look at the image again.
The red crosshair is definitely a bit too low.
It's currently between "Profile" and "Settings".
"Profile" is above it.
The distance from the crosshair to the center of "Profile" is about 20-30 pixels up.
Let's refine.
Crosshair: (650, 381)
Target ("Profile"): (650, 355) approx.
dx = 0, dy = -26.
Let's double check.
The crosshair is at 381. The line separating Profile and Settings is at maybe 370.
The "Profile" text center is around 355.
So dy is about -26.
dx seems to be 0 as the crosshair is horizontally centered on the text.
